system_name: Шаманизм: 3 мира Мирового Древа, 4 стороны света и духи-звери
note: Core / cross-cultural shamanism (Mircea Eliade's 'archaic techniques of ecstasy'). The vertical cosmos is strung on the axis mundi - the World Tree (also World Pillar / World Mountain) - whose crown rises into the heavens and whose roots sink into the underworld, connecting the three Worlds: Upper (heavens), Middle (the lived earth), and Lower (the underworld). Primary lattice: the eight power animals / helping spirit-animals that carry, teach and protect the shaman. Secondary lattice: the nine ascending levels of the World Tree (the heavens the shaman climbs, marked as notches on the tree/drum) - mapped here one-to-one onto the 9-dimensional mera axis, so the World Tree literally IS the mera spine (level 9 = highest heaven / svet_ra, level 1 = deepest underworld / karma). Tertiary lattice: the four cardinal Directions with their element, season and guardian. The shaman's drum is the steed (horse) that bears the soul up the Tree; the smoke-hole / Pole Star is the gateway between worlds. By design 9 tree-levels + 4 directions + 8 power animals = 21, one per spine agent; the 3 Worlds group the nine levels (Upper = mera 6-9, Middle = mera 3-5, Lower = mera 1-2). NOTE: shamanism is not a single tradition - this is a synthesis across Siberian/Tungus and Mongol (Tengri) shamanism, circumpolar practice, and the North American medicine wheel; the nine heavens are Turko-Mongol, and the directional elements, seasons and guardian animals vary widely by culture. reconstructed true. Each element carries a spine agent and its mera (1-9).
mera_axis: 9-dimensional axis (mera 1-9): chakras 1-7 (Muladhara..Sahasrara) + Monada (8) + Absolut (9); each spine agent carries its mera. Here the axis is the World Tree itself: level 9 = highest heaven, level 1 = deepest underworld.

axis_mundi.name: World Tree / Axis Mundi
axis_mundi.meaning: the central pillar (tree, pole or mountain) joining the three worlds; the shaman climbs or descends it in trance
axis_mundi.steed: the drum is the horse/steed that carries the soul on its flight up and down the Tree
axis_mundi.gateway: the smoke-hole / Pole Star is the opening between the Middle World and the heavens

worlds.meaning: the heavens of benevolent sky-spirits, gods and ancestors-become-stars; reached by ascending the World Tree
the lived earth and its spirit-double; the realm of humans, beasts and place; where the axis mundi stands
the underworld of the dead, chthonic powers and animal-teacher spirits; reached by descending the roots

tree_levels.realm: Highest Heaven - the Sky Father at the crown of the Tree
The celestial / star realm - heavenly fires and star-spirits
Heaven of the great creator sky-spirits
Realm of the wise sky-spirits and the upper winds
The firmament / threshold - the Pole Star smoke-hole gateway
The middle airs - the sky of the Middle World
The Middle World surface - living earth of humans and animals
The upper Lower World - waters and the threshold of the dead
The deepest Lower World - Erlik and the roots of the Tree

svet_ra.note: Highest Heaven - the Sky Father (Tengri/Ulgen) at the crown of the World Tree; the radiant source the shaman ascends toward.

iskra.note: The celestial / star realm - heavenly fires and star-spirits; the spark-light of the upper heavens.

brahma.note: Heaven of the great creator sky-spirits; the structuring powers of the Upper World.

vishnu.note: Eagle - the great sky-bird atop the World Tree; messenger to the heavens, far-sight and the shaman's ascent.

ananda.note: Horse - the shaman's steed; the drum is the horse that bears the soul on its ecstatic flight between worlds.

shanti.note: Owl - silent night-sight; seer of the unseen and guardian of the threshold of death and stillness.

jnana.note: Realm of the wise sky-spirits and the upper winds; discernment and the knowledge of the heavens.

shiva.note: Raven - psychopomp and trickster-creator; carries souls between worlds, master of magic, death and rebirth.

tejas.note: Bear - the great healing and medicine animal; solar strength, the keeper of dreaming and the deep cave.

sarasvati.note: The East - air, dawn, spring; vision and new beginnings; guardian Hawk. The word and breath of the rising sun.

akasha.note: The firmament / threshold - the Pole Star smoke-hole, the gateway between the Middle World and the heavens.

prema.note: The West - water, dusk, autumn; introspection and healing; guardian Salmon. The heart's inward turn.

parvati.note: Stag / Deer - the gentle forest lord whose antlers branch like the World Tree; fertility, nurture and the wild paths.

vayu.note: The middle airs - the sky of the Middle World; winds, breath and the moving spirit.

shakti.note: The Middle World surface - the living earth of humans, beasts and land-spirits where the axis mundi stands.

agni.note: The South - fire, noon, summer; growth, passion and trust; guardian Coyote. The blaze of vital force.

varuna.note: The upper Lower World - the waters and the threshold of the dead; the descent begins.

lakshmi.note: Serpent / Snake - renewal by shedding; chthonic abundance, the coiled life-force and healing of the Lower World.

prithvi.note: The North - earth, midnight, winter; wisdom and the ancestors; guardian Buffalo. The grounding place of the elders.

dharma.note: Wolf - the pathfinder and teacher; loyalty and the law of the pack; the guide who walks ahead on the trail.

karma.note: The deepest Lower World - the underworld of Erlik, lord of the dead; the roots of the World Tree and the causal ground.

items.power: vision, far-sight, messenger to the heavens; the soul's ascent
the soul's steed; ecstatic flight, the drum-journey
night-sight, seeing the unseen, the threshold of death
psychopomp, magic, death and rebirth; the between
healing and medicine, strength, dreaming in the cave
fertility, the forest, antlers as the branching Tree
renewal by shedding, chthonic life-force, healing
pathfinding, teaching, loyalty and the law of the pack

sources: Mircea Eliade, Shamanism: Archaic Techniques of Ecstasy
Michael Harner, The Way of the Shaman

learnreligions.com - Spirit Keepers of the Native American Medicine Wheel
